addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# General Information

New release of library to enhance readibility and unifying code through all files, new documentation organization.
This version is not backward compatible but a script is given to help user to adapt code to new release.

## Maintainer
Benoît Stef [benoit.stef@psi.ch]

## Authors
* Oliver Bründler [oli.bruendler@gmx.ch]
* Benoît Stef [benoit.stef@psi.ch]
* Daniele Felici [daniele.felici@psi.ch]
* Patric Bucher [patric.bucher@psi.ch]
* Rafael Basso [rafael.basso@psi.ch]
* Radoslaw Rybaniec [radoslaw.rybaniec@psi.ch]

## License
This library is published under [PSI HDL Library License](License.txt), which is [LGPL](LGPL2_1.txt) plus some additional exceptions to clarify the LGPL terms in the context of firmware development.

## Detailed Documentation
See [Documentation](doc/README.md)

## Changelog
See [Changelog](Changelog.md)

## What belongs into this Library
This library contains general VHDL code that is not very application specific and can be reused easily.
Code must be written with reuse in mind. All important settings must be implemented as Generics (e.g. port widths,
FIFO depths, etc,).

It is suggested to use one .vhd file per Package or Entity.

Examples for things that belong into this library:
* Clock-Crossings
* FIFOs
* Vendor Independent RAM implementations
* Packages that extend the language

## What does not belong into this Library

 * Any project specific code
 * Code that better fits into another library (e.g. signal processing code using psi_fix belongs into psi_fix)
 * Code that is not fully parametrizable

## Tagging Policy
Stable releases are tagged in the form *major*.*minor*.*bugfix*.

* Whenever a change is not fully backward compatible, the *major* version number is incremented
* Whenever new features are added, the *minor* version number is incremented
* If only bugs are fixed (i.e. no functional changes are applied), the *bugfix* version is incremented

# Dependencies

The required folder structure looks as given below (folder names must be matched exactly).

Alternatively the repository [psi\_fpga\_all](https://github.com/paulscherrerinstitute/psi_fpga_all) can be used. This repo contains all FPGA related repositories as submodules in the correct folder structure.
* TCL
  * [PsiSim](https://github.com/paulscherrerinstitute/PsiSim) (2.1.0 or higher)
* VHDL
  * [**psi\_common**](https://github.com/paulscherrerinstitute/psi_common)
  * [psi\_tb](https://github.com/paulscherrerinstitute/psi_tb) (3.0.0 or higher)

<!-- END OF PARSED SECTION -->

Dependencies can also be checked out using the python script *scripts/dependencies.py*. For details, refer to the help of the script:

```
python dependencies.py -help
```

Note that the [dependencies package](https://github.com/paulscherrerinstitute/PsiFpgaLibDependencies) must be installed in order to run the script.

# Simulations and Testbenches

For everything that is non-trivial, self-checking testbenches shall be provided to allow easy and safe reuse of
the library elements.

A regression test script for Modelsim is present. New Testbenches must therefore be added to the configuration of the
regression test script *sim/config.tcl*.

To run the regression test, execute the following command in modelsim from within the directory *sim*

```
source ./run.tcl
```

The regression tests can also be ran using GHDL. To do so, GHDL must be installed and added to the path and a TCL interpreter must be installed. In the TCL interpreter, execute the following command within the directory *sim*:

```
source ./runGhdl.tcl
```

1 2 3 *** 4 [Back](../README.md) 5 6 # Introduction 7 8 ## 1.1 Working Copy Structure 9 If you just want to use some components out of the *psi\_common* 10 library, no special structure is required and the repository can be used 11 standalone. 12 13 If you want to also run simulations and/or modify the library, 14 additional repositories are required (available from the same source as 15 *psi\_common*) and they must be checked out into the folder structure 16 shown in the figure below since the repositories reference each-other 17 relatively. 18 19 It is not necessary but recommended to use the name *psi\_lib* as name 20 for the *\* folder. 21 22

23 24

25 26 ## 1.2 VHDL Libraries 27 28 PSI VHDL libraries (including psi_common) require all files to be compiled into the same VHDL library. 29 There are two common ways of using VHDL libraries when using PSI VHDL libraries: 30 - All files of the project (including project specific sources and PSI VHDL library sources) are compiled into the same library that may have any name. 31 In this case PSI library entities and packages are referenced by **work.psi_[library]_[xxx]** (e.g. work.psi_common_pl_stage or work.psi_common_array_pkg.all). 32 - All code from PSI VHDL libraries is compiled into a separate VHDL library. It is recommended to use the name psi_lib. 33 In this case PSI library entities and packages are referenced by **psi_lib.psi_[lib]_[xxx]** (e.g. psi_lib.psi_common_pl_stage or psi_lib.psi_common_array_pkg.all). 34 35 36 ## 1.3 Running simulations 37 38 ### 1.3.1 Regression Test 39 #### 1.3.1.1 Modelsim 40 To run the regression test, follow the steps below: 41 - Open Modelsim 42 - The TCL console, navigate to /VHDL/psi_common/sim 43 - Execute the command “source ./run.tcl” 44 All test benches are executed automatically and at the end of the regression test, the result is reported 45 46 47 #### 1.3.1.2 GHDL 48 In order to run the regression tests using GHDL, GHDL must be installed and added to the path variable. Additionally a TCL interpreter must be installed. 49 To run the regression tests using GHDL, follow the steps below: 50 - Open the TCL interpreter (usually by running tclsh) 51 - The TCL console, navigate to /VHDL/psi_common/sim 52 - Execute the command **“source ./runGhdl.tcl”** 53 All test benches are executed automatically and at the end of the regression test, the result is reported 54 55 ### 1.3.2 Working Interactively 56 57 During work on library components, it is important to be able to control simulations interactively. To do so, it is suggested to follow the following flow: 58 * Open Modelsim 59 * The TCL console, navigate to /VHDL/psi_common/sim 60 * Execute the command **“source ./interactive.tcl”** 61 * This will compile all files and initialize the PSI TCL framework 62 * From this point on, all the commands from the PSI TCL framework are available, see documentation of PsiSim 63 * Most useful commands to recompile and simulate entities selectively are 64 * **_compile_files –contains [string]_** 65 * **_run_tb –contains [string]_** 66 * **_launch_tb –contains [string]_** 67 68 The steps for GHDL are the same, just in the TCL interpreter shall instead of the Modelsim TCL console. 69 70 71 ## 1.4 Contribute to PSI VHDL Libraries 72 To contribute to the PSI VHDL libraries, a few rules must be followed: 73 * **Good Code Quality** 74 * There are not hard guidelines. However, your code shall be readable, understandable, correct and save. In other words: Only good code quality will be accepted. 75 76 * **Configurability** 77 * If there are parameters that other users may have to modify at compile-time, provide generics. Only code that is written in a generic way and can easily be reused will be accepted. 78 79 * **Self-checking Test-benches** 80 * It is mandatory to provide a self-checking test-bench with your code. 81 The test-bench shall cover all features of your code 82 * The test-bench shall automatically stop after it is completed (all processes halted, clock-generation stopped). See existing test-benches provided with the library for examples. 83 * The test-bench shall only do reports of severity error, failure or even fatal if there is a real problem. 84 * If an error occurs, the message reported shall start with “###ERROR###:”. This is required since the regression test script searches for this string in reports. 85 86 * **Documentation** 87 * Extend this document with proper documentation of your code. 88 89 * **New test-benches** must be added to the regression test-script 90 * Change /sim/config.tcl accordingly 91 * Test if the regression test really runs the new test-bench and exits without errors before doing any merge requests. 92 93 * **GIT commit annotations**, please add a short description at first of your commit annotation, this ease the maintainer to merge, write the changelog.md file while doing new release and others to understand what has been committed, here below there are 6 inputs that are commonly use. 94 * **_FEATURE_**: Adding a new feature to library like a component or a package element (i.e. function, procedure, type and constant) 95 * **_GIT_**: when committing some GIT related issue, merging, etc… 96 * **_BUGFIX_**: when a fix has been made 97 * **_DOCU_**: documentation related comment 98 * **_DEVEL_**: when a commit is done but work is in development 99 * **_TB_**: test bench related commit 100 101 7. **Working with GIT** 102 * If a user wants to participate to the library he is free to do so, however some rules should be considered. All PSI libraries have at least two branches develop & master. The master branch is used for stable release version all changes are merge to master when required. The develop branch is the branch when a GIT user shall diverge from to add a new component. 103 * A good practice is to call the branch the name of the new block, the user is free to push into this branch and once the work is over a pull request can be done to the base branch develop (Step 1). Members of the library may then have exchange and add comment during a code review (Step 2). The initial user takes into consideration the comment and once bug are fixed for instance a new pull request can be done. If members agree on the new feature then it is merged to develop (Step 3) and the branch will be safely deleted by the repository maintainer. 104 105

106 107

108 109 ## 1.5 Handshaking Signals 110 ### 1.5.1 General Information 111 The PSI library uses the AXI4-Stream handshaking protocol (herein after called AXI-S). Not all entities may implement all optional features of the AXI-S standard (e.g. backpressure may be omitted) but the features available are implemented according to AXI-S standard and follow these rules. 112 The full AXI-S specification can be downloaded from the ARM homepage: 113 https://developer.arm.com/docs/ihi0051/a 114 The most important points of the specification are outlined below. 115 ### 1.5.2 Excerpt of the AXI-S Standard 116 A data transfer takes place during a clock cycle where TVALID and TREADY (if available) are high. The order in which they are asserted does not play any role. 117 - A master is not permitted to wait until TREADY is asserted before asserting TVALID. 118 - Once TVALID is asserted it must remain asserted until the handshake occurs. 119 - A slave is permitted to wait for TVALID to be asserted before asserting the corresponding TREADY. 120 - If a slave asserts TREADY, it is permitted to de-assert TREADY before TVALID is asserted. 121 An example an AXI handshaking waveform is given below. All the points where data is actually transferred are marked with dashed lines. 122 123

124 125

126 127 ### 1.5.3 Naming 128 The naming conventions of the AXI-S standard are not followed strictly. The most common synonyms that can be found within the PSI VHDL libraries are described below: 129 - TDATA InData, OutData, Data, Sig, Signal, 130 - TVALID Vld, InVld, OutVld, Valid, str, str_i 131 - TREADY Rdy, InRdy, OutRdy 132 133 Note that instead of one TDATA signal (as specified by AXI-S) the PSI VHDL Library sometimes has multiple data signals that are all related to the same set of handshaking signals. This helps with readability since different data can is represented by different signals instead of just one large vector. 134 135 ## 1.6 TDM 136 137 **Rules** 138 139 - If multiple signals are transferred TDM (time-division-multiplexed) over the same interface and all signals have the same sample rate, no additional channel indicator is implemented and looping through the channels is implicit (e.g. for a three channel link, samples are transferred in the channel order 0-1-2-0-1-2-…). 140 141 - If multiple signals are transferred TDM over the same interface and the channels do not have the same sample rate, an additional channel indicator containing the channel number is present. 142 143 **Reasoning** 144 145 Not having an additional channel indicator for the most common TDM use-case of multiple signals at the same sample rate prevents any combinatorial blocks from having to know about being used for a TDM signal and maintaining the channel indicator. This implicitly allows using all combinatorial library elements (e.g. binary divider, function approximations, etc.) also for TDM signals 146 147 *** 148 149 [Back](../README.md) 150

### 1.5.3 Naming The naming conventions of the AXI-S standard are not followed strictly. The most common synonyms that can be found within the PSI VHDL libraries are described below: - TDATA InData, OutData, Data, Sig, Signal, - TVALID Vld, InVld, OutVld, Valid, str, str_i - TREADY Rdy, InRdy, OutRdy Note that instead of one TDATA signal (as specified by AXI-S) the PSI VHDL Library sometimes has multiple data signals that are all related to the same set of handshaking signals. This helps with readability since different data can is represented by different signals instead of just one large vector. ## 1.6 TDM **Rules** - If multiple signals are transferred TDM (time-division-multiplexed) over the same interface and all signals have the same sample rate, no additional channel indicator is implemented and looping through the channels is implicit (e.g. for a three channel link, samples are transferred in the channel order 0-1-2-0-1-2-…). - If multiple signals are transferred TDM over the same interface and the channels do not have the same sample rate, an additional channel indicator containing the channel number is present. **Reasoning** Not having an additional channel indicator for the most common TDM use-case of multiple signals at the same sample rate prevents any combinatorial blocks from having to know about being used for a TDM signal and maintaining the channel indicator. This implicitly allows using all combinatorial library elements (e.g. binary divider, function approximations, etc.) also for TDM signals *** [Back](../README.md)

2	--  Copyright (c) 2018 by Paul Scherrer Institute, Switzerland

4	--  Authors: Oliver Bruendler
5	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6	
7	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8	-- Description
9	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10	-- This is a very basic clock crossing that allows passing single samples of data
11	-- from one clock domain to another. It only works if sample rates are significantly
12	-- lower than the clock speed of both domains.
13	
14	library ieee;
15	use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
16	use ieee.numeric_std.all;
17	
18	-- @formatter:off
19	entity psi_common_simple_cc is
20	  generic(width_g      : positive := 16;                                  --  Width of the data signal to implement the clock crossing for
21	          a_rst_pol_g  : std_logic:='1';                                  --  reset polarity A port
22	          b_rst_pol_g  : std_logic:='1');                                 --  reset polarity B port
23	  port(   a_clk_i      : in  std_logic;                                   --  Clock A
24	          a_rst_i      : in  std_logic;                                   --  Clock domain A reset input (active high)
25	          a_rst_o      : out std_logic;                                   --  Clock domain A reset output (active high), active if *RstInA* or *RstInB* is asserted, de-asserted synchronously to *ClkA*
26	          a_dat_i      : in  std_logic_vector(width_g - 1 downto 0);      --  Data signal input
27	          a_vld_i      : in  std_logic;                                   --  AXI-S handshaking signal
28	          b_clk_i      : in  std_logic;                                   --  Clock B
29	          b_rst_i      : in  std_logic;                                   --  Clock domain A reset input (active high)
30	          b_rst_o      : out std_logic;                                   --  Clock domain B reset output (active high), active if *RstInA* or *RstInB* is asserted, de-asserted synchronously to *ClkA*
31	          b_dat_o      : out std_logic_vector(width_g - 1 downto 0);      --  Data signal output
32	          b_vld_o      : out std_logic);                                  --  AXI-S handshaking signal
33	end entity;
34	-- @formatter:on
35	
36	architecture rtl of psi_common_simple_cc is
37	  -- Domain A signals
38	  signal RstAI      : std_logic;
39	  signal DataLatchA : std_logic_vector(width_g - 1 downto 0);
40	  -- Domain B signals
41	  signal RstBI      : std_logic;
42	  signal VldBI      : std_logic;
43	
44	begin
45	
46	  i_pulse_cc : entity work.psi_common_pulse_cc
47	    generic map(
48	      num_pulses_g => 1,
49	      a_rst_pol_g => a_rst_pol_g,
50	      b_rst_pol_g => b_rst_pol_g
51	    )
52	    port map(
53	      a_clk_i    => a_clk_i,
54	      a_rst_i    => a_rst_i,
55	      a_rst_o    => RstAI,
56	      a_dat_i(0) => a_vld_i,
57	      b_clk_i    => b_clk_i,
58	      b_rst_i    => b_rst_i,
59	      b_rst_o    => RstBI,
60	      b_dat_o(0) => VldBI
61	    );
62	  a_rst_o <= RstAI;
63	  b_rst_o <= RstBI;
64	
65	  -- Data transmit side (A)
66	  DataA_p : process(a_clk_i)
67	  begin
68	    if rising_edge(a_clk_i) then
69	      if RstAI = a_rst_pol_g then
70	        DataLatchA <= (others => '0');
71	      else
72	        if a_vld_i = '1' then
73	          DataLatchA <= a_dat_i;
74	        end if;
75	      end if;
76	    end if;
77	  end process;
78	
79	  -- Data receive side (B)
80	  DataB_p : process(b_clk_i)
81	  begin
82	    if rising_edge(b_clk_i) then
83	      if RstBI = b_rst_pol_g then
84	        b_dat_o <= (others => '0');
85	        b_vld_o <= '0';
86	      else
87	        b_vld_o <= VldBI;
88	        if VldBI = '1' then
89	          b_dat_o <= DataLatchA;
90	        end if;
91	      end if;
92	    end if;
93	  end process;
94	
95	end architecture;
96

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- This is a very basic asynchronous FIFO. The clocks can be fully asynchronous
-- (unrelated). It  has optional level- and almost-full/empty ports.

library ieee;
use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
use ieee.numeric_std.all;

use work.psi_common_logic_pkg.all;
use work.psi_common_math_pkg.all;
--@formatter:off
entity psi_common_async_fifo is
  generic(width_g         : positive  := 16;                                         -- Width of the FIFO|
          depth_g         : positive  := 32;                                         -- Depth of the FIFO|
          afull_on_g      : boolean   := false;                                      -- **True** = Almost full output is provided, **False** = Almost full output is omitted|
          afull_lvl_g     : natural   := 28;                                         -- Almost full output is high if the level is \>= AlmFullLevel\_g|
          aempty_on_g     : boolean   := false;                                      -- True = Almost empty output is provided, False = Almost empty output is omitted|
          aempty_level_g  : natural   := 4;                                          -- Almost empty output is high if the level is \<= AlmFullLevel\_g|

Usually this does not play a role and the default setting ('1') that leads to the least logic on the InRdy path is fine.           out_dat_o    : out std_logic_vector(width_g - 1 downto 0);                  --    Read data

          out_aempty_o : out std_logic;                                               --    FIFO almost   empty signal  synchronous to *out_clk_i*  Only exists if  *AlmEmptyOn\_g* = true
          out_lvl_o    : out std_logic_vector(log2ceil(depth_g + 1) - 1 downto 0));   --    FIFO level synchronous to   *out_clk_i*

input.rationale: psi_common 是 Paul Scherrer Institute 维护的大型通用可复用 VHDL 库（60+ 组件），因此手册按"先上手、再打基础、后分领域、最后工程化"的顺序分为 11 个单元。U1 帮助读者从零认识项目定位、目录结构、依赖与仿真方式以及贯穿全库的编码规范/AXI-S 握手/TDM 约定；U2 集中讲解被所有组件复用的四个基础包（math/logic/array/axi）；U3-U4 从底层存储 RAM 一路向上到同步/异步 FIFO，建立"存储→缓冲"的依赖链；U5-U6 讲解 CDC 跨时钟域与时序节拍生成，它们是后续数据通路组件的前置；U7 讲解以二进程 record 法为核心的流水线/延迟/乒乓缓冲；U8 在流水线基础上展开宽度转换、TDM 与并串转换；U9 进入较复杂的总线接口（SPI/I2C/AXI），依赖 axi_pkg 与流水线；U10 汇总仲裁器、脉冲/斜坡生成、实用小组件与统计/信号源等杂项组件；U11 收尾于工程化主题——自检测试平台、Python 代码生成器、重构迁移脚本与贡献/发布流程。讲义依赖关系严格按"基础包→存储→FIFO→CDC→时序→流水线→数据通路→接口→杂项→工程化"递进，确保每篇只讲一个独立主题、引用 1-6 个真实源码文件，并配一个可操作的代码实践任务。

lectures.title: 项目概览与定位
lectures.topic: 介绍 psi_common 是 Paul Scherrer Institute 维护的通用可复用 VHDL 库，说明它解决什么问题、许可方式、维护者与适用场景，帮助读者建立整体认知。
learning_goals: 理解 psi_common 的定位与价值
了解库的许可（PSI HDL License / LGPL）与维护者
知道库中包含哪些类别的可复用组件
lectures.practice_task: 浏览 README 与 doc/README 的组件表，列出你最可能用到的 5 个组件并各写一句用途。

lectures.title: 仓库结构与目录组织
lectures.topic: 讲解仓库顶层目录划分（hdl/testbench/sim/doc/scripts/generators/sigasi），说明每个目录存放什么、源码与测试如何一一对应。
learning_goals: 掌握仓库目录布局
理解 hdl 源码与 testbench 的命名对应关系
知道文档、脚本、生成器的所在位置
lectures.practice_task: 统计 hdl/ 下源文件数量，并为其中 3 个组件找到对应的 testbench 目录。

lectures.title: 依赖管理与仿真运行
lectures.topic: 说明 psi_common 对 PsiSim/psi_tb 的依赖与目录结构要求，讲解如何用 Modelsim 或 GHDL 运行回归测试与交互仿真。
learning_goals: 理解库的依赖关系与目录结构要求
学会运行 Modelsim/GHDL 回归测试
掌握交互仿真命令
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 sim/config.tcl，找到 pl_stage 组件的 testbench 注册条目并说明其 generic 参数。

lectures.title: 编码规范、AXI-S 握手与 TDM 约定
lectures.topic: 讲解库的代码规范（snake_case、_i/_o/_io 后缀、架构命名）、AXI4-Stream 握手（VLD/RDY）语义以及 TDM 数据流的隐式通道循环约定。
learning_goals: 掌握库的命名与编码规范
理解 AXI-S VLD/RDY 握手规则
理解 TDM 通道隐式循环约定
minimal_modules: 规范在 pl_stage 中的体现
lectures.practice_task: 在 pl_stage.vhd 中找出符合规范的端口命名，并解释 use_rdy_g 如何影响握手行为。

lectures.title: psi_common_math_pkg 数学工具函数
lectures.topic: 讲解数学包中的编译期函数（log2/log2ceil、max/min、choose、ratio、to_uslv、nearest_pow2 等），这些函数广泛用于推导端口宽度与常量。
learning_goals: 掌握 log2/log2ceil 推导位宽的方法
理解 choose 的多态重载
学会整数与 std_logic_vector 的相互转换
lectures.practice_task: 用 log2ceil 计算深度 1024 的 RAM 地址位宽，并用 to_uslv 将一个整数转为 std_logic_vector。

lectures.title: psi_common_logic_pkg 逻辑向量工具
lectures.topic: 讲解逻辑包中的向量生成、移位、格雷码转换、并行前缀或（ppc_or）与归约函数，它们在 CDC 与 FIFO 中经常被复用。
learning_goals: 掌握 zeros/ones/shift 向量工具
理解二进制-格雷码转换
学会 reduce_or/reduce_and 归约
lectures.practice_task: 用 binary_to_gray/gray_to_binary 实现地址的格雷编解码并验证可逆性。

lectures.title: psi_common_array_pkg 数组类型
lectures.topic: 讲解数组包中预定义的 t_aslvN/t_ainteger/t_areal/t_abool 等类型，说明它们为何在 VHDL-2008 全面可综合前仍然必要。
learning_goals: 了解预定义数组类型的命名规律
理解 t_aslv 无约束数组与约束数组的区别
知道在何处使用这些类型
lectures.practice_task: 声明一个 t_aslv16 数组存放 4 个 16 位常数，并用 math_pkg 的 count 统计某比特出现次数。

lectures.title: psi_common_axi_pkg AXI 记录类型
lectures.topic: 讲解 AXI 包如何用 record 把完整 AXI 接口收敛为两个方向的记录（rec_axi_ms/rec_axi_sm），提升带 AXI 接口实体的可读性。
learning_goals: 理解 AXI 五通道的 record 建模
掌握 ms/sm 方向命名约定
知道默认常量与遗留 subtype
lectures.practice_task: 参照包中用法，声明一个 AXI 从机端口的 ms/sm 记录对并赋默认常量。

lectures.title: 简单双口 RAM：sdp_ram 与 sp_ram_be
lectures.topic: 讲解简单双口 RAM 的纯 VHDL 实现，包括 ram_style 综合属性、读前写/写前读（RBW/WBR）行为以及字节使能变体。
learning_goals: 理解 depth/width 与地址位宽关系
掌握 ram_style 与 RBW/WBR 行为差异
了解 shared variable 存储建模
lectures.practice_task: 实例化一个 512x16 的 sdp_ram，用 log2ceil 推导地址位宽，并说明 RBW 与 WBR 的输出差异。

lectures.title: 真双口 RAM：tdp_ram 与 tdp_ram_be
lectures.topic: 讲解真双口 RAM 的两个独立读写端口及其字节使能变体，说明它如何作为异步 FIFO 与乒乓缓冲的底层存储。
learning_goals: 理解真双口 RAM 的双端口模型
掌握字节使能写入
了解其作为跨时钟存储介质的作用
lectures.practice_task: 比较 sdp_ram 与 tdp_ram 的端口，说明跨时钟场景下为何应选 tdp_ram。

lectures.title: 同步 FIFO：sync_fifo
lectures.topic: 讲解同步 FIFO 的读写指针、满/空与几乎满/空标志、电平输出，以及它在单时钟域数据缓冲中的应用。
learning_goals: 理解 FIFO 满空判断原理
掌握 alm_full/alm_empty 与 level 输出
学会 AXI-S 接口的 FIFO 用法
lectures.practice_task: 实例化 sync_fifo，设计小场景观察 alm_full 在 depth=32 时的触发时刻。

lectures.title: 异步 FIFO：async_fifo 与格雷码指针
lectures.topic: 讲解异步 FIFO 如何用格雷码指针跨时钟域传递、如何区分满与空，以及多级寄存器同步与二进程 record 设计法。
learning_goals: 理解格雷码指针的 CDC 作用
掌握满/空判别（多一位指针）
了解二进程 record 架构
lectures.practice_task: 跟踪 async_fifo 中 WrAddrGray 到 RdAddrGraySync 的同步路径，解释为何需要格雷码。

lectures.title: 脉冲跨越 pulse_cc 与复位同步
lectures.topic: 讲解脉冲跨时钟域的

lectures.title: 数据/状态/位跨越 simple_cc/status_cc/bit_cc
lectures.topic: 讲解在 pulse_cc 之上构建的数据值跨越（simple_cc）、慢变状态跨越（status_cc）与单 bit 跨越（bit_cc）的设计与取舍。
learning_goals: 理解 simple_cc 复用 pulse_cc 的设计
区分脉冲/状态/位跨越的适用场景
学会复位输出传递
lectures.practice_task: 为

lectures.title: 同步整数比跨越 sync_cc_n2xn / sync_cc_xn2n
lectures.topic: 讲解两个同步（整数倍频）时钟域之间、带 AXI-S 握手的跨越，包括低到高（n2xn）与高到低（xn2n）两种方向。
learning_goals: 理解整数比时钟的同步跨越原理
掌握 n2xn/xn2n 的方向差异
了解 ratio 参数与节拍关系
lectures.practice_task: 给定 100MHz 与 25MHz 两个同步时钟，选择正确组件并预测数据聚合/分发行为。

lectures.title: 选通与节拍生成
lectures.topic: 讲解按频率生成单周期选通的 strobe_generator、按周期数可配置的 strobe_generator_cfg、节拍 tickgenerator 以及选通分频 strobe_divider。
learning_goals: 理解频率到计数比的换算
掌握 sync 同步输入
区分固定频率与可配置周期生成
理解选通分频
lectures.practice_task: 在 100MHz 时钟下用 strobe_generator 生成 1kHz 选通，再用 strobe_divider 分频 10 倍。

lectures.title: 时钟测量 clk_meas
lectures.topic: 讲解如何用已知参考时钟测量另一时钟（或选通）的频率，输出可读的频率/周期值。
learning_goals: 理解参考时钟计数测频原理
掌握 clk_meas 的接口与输出
了解其在系统自检中的用途
lectures.practice_task: 设计用 100MHz 参考测量一个未知时钟，并估算测量精度。

lectures.title: 流水线级 pl_stage 与二进程设计法
lectures.topic: 以 pl_stage 为例讲解带 AXI-S 握手的流水线级、影子寄存器处理反压，以及 PSI 库常用的二进程（record r/r_next）设计法。
learning_goals: 掌握二进程 record 设计法
理解影子寄存器如何处理反压
学会 use_rdy_g 的两种实现
lectures.practice_task: 在 pl_stage 中跟踪一次下游反压的数据流动，画出主/影寄存器状态。

lectures.title: 多级流水线 multi_pl_stage
lectures.topic: 讲解 multi_pl_stage 如何把多个 pl_stage 串联以打更深流水线，并统一处理握手与反压。
learning_goals: 理解多级流水线的级联结构
掌握 stages generic
了解反压在多级中的传递
lectures.practice_task: 实例化一个 4 级 multi_pl_stage，预测连续数据下的延迟周期数。

lectures.title: 可配置延迟 delay / delay_cfg
lectures.topic: 讲解固定延迟（delay，支持 BRAM/SRL/AUTO）与运行时可配置延迟（delay_cfg）的实现与资源选择。
learning_goals: 理解延迟线的存储实现
掌握 BRAM/SRL 资源选择
了解可配置延迟的寄存器接口
lectures.practice_task: 比较 delay 在 BRAM 与 SRL 两种 resource_g 下、不同 delay_g 的资源占用思路。

lectures.title: 乒乓缓冲 ping_pong
lectures.topic: 讲解乒乓缓冲如何用双 RAM 交替写入/读出实现连续数据流缓冲，及其 TDM 突发变体。
learning_goals: 理解乒乓双缓冲原理
掌握写/读切换逻辑
了解 TDM 突发应用

lectures.title: 数据宽度转换 wconv_n2xn / wconv_xn2n
lectures.topic: 讲解 N 位与整数倍 N 位之间的数据宽度转换，包括低到高聚合与高到低分发。
learning_goals: 理解宽度聚合/分发的握手时序
掌握 ratio 参数
了解与 sync_cc 的区别
lectures.practice_task: 把 8 位流聚合成 32 位流，预测输出 valid 的频率变化。

lectures.title: 并-TDM 与 TDM-并 par_tdm / tdm_par
lectures.topic: 讲解并行数据与时分复用（TDM）串行流之间的相互转换，以及隐式通道循环约定。
learning_goals: 理解 TDM 通道隐式循环
掌握 par_tdm/tdm_par 转换时序
了解 strobe 驱动
lectures.practice_task: 将 4 路并行 16 位数据转为 TDM 流并说明通道顺序。

lectures.title: 可配置 TDM 与多路复用 cfg/tdm_mux
lectures.topic: 讲解可配置有效通道数的 par_tdm_cfg/tdm_par_cfg，以及 TDM 多路复用器 tdm_mux。
learning_goals: 理解可配置通道数的 TDM 转换
掌握 tdm_mux 的复用与 str_del
了解代码生成器辅助
lectures.practice_task: 用 tdm_mux 把两路 TDM 流合并，并解释 str_del_g 的作用。

lectures.title: 并串/串并转换 par_ser / ser_par
lectures.topic: 讲解并行到串行与串行到并行的移位转换，包括 MSB/LSB 优先与速率比参数。
learning_goals: 理解并串/串并移位原理
掌握 msb/ratio 参数
了解与 strobe 的配合
lectures.practice_task: 用 par_ser 把 16 位并行数据按 MSB 优先、ratio=4 串行化。

lectures.title: SPI 主机 spi_master 与 spi_master_cfg
lectures.topic: 讲解 SPI 主机的时序（CPOL/CPHA）、位序与可配置位宽变体，以及三线 SPI 接口（spi_tri_o）。
learning_goals: 理解 CPOL/CPHA 四种模式
掌握 lsb_first 与位宽配置
了解三线 SPI 端口
lectures.practice_task: 配置 spi_master 为模式 1、MSB 优先，说明 SCLK 与片选时序。

lectures.title: I2C 主机 i2c_master
lectures.topic: 讲解 I2C 主机的 START/STOP/ACK 时序、时钟分频与内部三态选项。
learning_goals: 理解 I2C 协议帧时序
掌握时钟分频配置
了解 internal_tri_state 选项
lectures.practice_task: 描述一次 I2C 单字节写传输的 SDA/SCL 波形。

lectures.title: AXI 简化主机 axi_master_simple
lectures.topic: 讲解简化版 AXI4 主机如何把用户侧简单的命令/数据接口映射到 AXI 四通道握手。
learning_goals: 理解 AXI 四通道时序

了解突发与 throttling
lectures.practice_task: 用 axi_master_simple 发起一次 4 拍突发读，画出握手时序。

lectures.title: AXI 完整主机 axi_master_full 与 axi_multi_pl_stage

掌握 multi_pl_stage 对 AXI 的时序优化

lectures.title: AXI 从机 axi_slave_ipif 与 axilite_slave_ipif

lectures.title: 仲裁器 arb_priority / arb_round_robin

lectures.title: 脉冲/斜坡生成与整形
lectures.topic: 讲解斜坡/脉冲发生器（ramp_gene、pulse_generator_ctrl_static）与脉冲整形器（pulse_shaper/cfg）的步进与定时参数。

lectures.title: 实用组件：看门狗/消抖/防优化/触发器/动态移位
lectures.topic: 讲解看门狗（watchdog）、消抖器（debouncer）、防综合优化（dont_opt）、模拟/数字触发生成（trigger_analog/digital）与动态移位器（dyn_sft）。

了解触发生成与动态移位
lectures.practice_task: 用 debouncer 处理一个按键输入，并选择合适的消抖周期。

lectures.title: 统计与信号源：min_max/prbs/pwm/sample_rate_converter
lectures.topic: 讲解 find_min_max/min_max_sum 流式统计、PRBS 伪随机序列、PWM 以及无滤波采样率转换器（sample_rate_converter）。

lectures.title: 编写自校验测试平台（psi_tb 用法）
lectures.topic: 以 pl_stage 测试平台为例，讲解 PSI 库自校验 TB 的结构、多进程协调、###ERROR### 约定与 psi_tb 工具包用法。

lectures.title: Python 代码生成器 generators

lectures.topic: 讲解向 PSI VHDL 库贡献代码的规范（代码质量、generic 化、自检 TB、文档）、git 分支策略、commit 注释约定与版本/标签管理。

了解版本号与标签策略

lectures.title: 脉冲跨越 pulse_cc 与复位同步
lectures.topic: 讲解脉冲跨时钟域的翻转-同步-异或手法、复位双向同步，以及 ASYNC_REG/syn_srlstyle 等综合约束。

掌握复位同步与极性处理

data.head: 98c2fcc75fa11edfe837cf0de885da95d94c871f
data.rationale: psi_common 是 Paul Scherrer Institute 维护的大型通用可复用 VHDL 库（60+ 组件），因此手册按"先上手、再打基础、后分领域、最后工程化"的顺序分为 11 个单元。U1 帮助读者从零认识项目定位、目录结构、依赖与仿真方式以及贯穿全库的编码规范/AXI-S 握手/TDM 约定；U2 集中讲解被所有组件复用的四个基础包（math/logic/array/axi）；U3-U4 从底层存储 RAM 一路向上到同步/异步 FIFO，建立"存储→缓冲"的依赖链；U5-U6 讲解 CDC 跨时钟域与时序节拍生成，它们是后续数据通路组件的前置；U7 讲解以二进程 record 法为核心的流水线/延迟/乒乓缓冲；U8 在流水线基础上展开宽度转换、TDM 与并串转换；U9 进入较复杂的总线接口（SPI/I2C/AXI），依赖 axi_pkg 与流水线；U10 汇总仲裁器、脉冲/斜坡生成、实用小组件与统计/信号源等杂项组件；U11 收尾于工程化主题——自检测试平台、Python 代码生成器、重构迁移脚本与贡献/发布流程。讲义依赖关系严格按"基础包→存储→FIFO→CDC→时序→流水线→数据通路→接口→杂项→工程化"递进，确保每篇只讲一个独立主题、引用 1-6 个真实源码文件，并配一个可操作的代码实践任务。

lectures.title: 项目概览与定位
lectures.topic: 介绍 psi_common 是 Paul Scherrer Institute 维护的通用可复用 VHDL 库，说明它解决什么问题、许可方式、维护者与适用场景，帮助读者建立整体认知。
learning_goals: 理解 psi_common 的定位与价值
了解库的许可（PSI HDL License / LGPL）与维护者
知道库中包含哪些类别的可复用组件
lectures.practice_task: 浏览 README 与 doc/README 的组件表，列出你最可能用到的 5 个组件并各写一句用途。

lectures.title: 仓库结构与目录组织
lectures.topic: 讲解仓库顶层目录划分（hdl/testbench/sim/doc/scripts/generators/sigasi），说明每个目录存放什么、源码与测试如何一一对应。
learning_goals: 掌握仓库目录布局
理解 hdl 源码与 testbench 的命名对应关系
知道文档、脚本、生成器的所在位置
lectures.practice_task: 统计 hdl/ 下源文件数量，并为其中 3 个组件找到对应的 testbench 目录。

lectures.title: 依赖管理与仿真运行
lectures.topic: 说明 psi_common 对 PsiSim/psi_tb 的依赖与目录结构要求，讲解如何用 Modelsim 或 GHDL 运行回归测试与交互仿真。
learning_goals: 理解库的依赖关系与目录结构要求
学会运行 Modelsim/GHDL 回归测试
掌握交互仿真命令
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 sim/config.tcl，找到 pl_stage 组件的 testbench 注册条目并说明其 generic 参数。

lectures.title: 编码规范、AXI-S 握手与 TDM 约定
lectures.topic: 讲解库的代码规范（snake_case、_i/_o/_io 后缀、架构命名）、AXI4-Stream 握手（VLD/RDY）语义以及 TDM 数据流的隐式通道循环约定。
learning_goals: 掌握库的命名与编码规范
理解 AXI-S VLD/RDY 握手规则
理解 TDM 通道隐式循环约定
minimal_modules: 规范在 pl_stage 中的体现
lectures.practice_task: 在 pl_stage.vhd 中找出符合规范的端口命名，并解释 use_rdy_g 如何影响握手行为。

lectures.title: psi_common_math_pkg 数学工具函数
lectures.topic: 讲解数学包中的编译期函数（log2/log2ceil、max/min、choose、ratio、to_uslv、nearest_pow2 等），这些函数广泛用于推导端口宽度与常量。
learning_goals: 掌握 log2/log2ceil 推导位宽的方法
理解 choose 的多态重载
学会整数与 std_logic_vector 的相互转换
lectures.practice_task: 用 log2ceil 计算深度 1024 的 RAM 地址位宽，并用 to_uslv 将一个整数转为 std_logic_vector。

lectures.title: psi_common_logic_pkg 逻辑向量工具
lectures.topic: 讲解逻辑包中的向量生成、移位、格雷码转换、并行前缀或（ppc_or）与归约函数，它们在 CDC 与 FIFO 中经常被复用。
learning_goals: 掌握 zeros/ones/shift 向量工具
理解二进制-格雷码转换
学会 reduce_or/reduce_and 归约
lectures.practice_task: 用 binary_to_gray/gray_to_binary 实现地址的格雷编解码并验证可逆性。

lectures.title: psi_common_array_pkg 数组类型
lectures.topic: 讲解数组包中预定义的 t_aslvN/t_ainteger/t_areal/t_abool 等类型，说明它们为何在 VHDL-2008 全面可综合前仍然必要。
learning_goals: 了解预定义数组类型的命名规律
理解 t_aslv 无约束数组与约束数组的区别
知道在何处使用这些类型
lectures.practice_task: 声明一个 t_aslv16 数组存放 4 个 16 位常数，并用 math_pkg 的 count 统计某比特出现次数。

lectures.title: psi_common_axi_pkg AXI 记录类型
lectures.topic: 讲解 AXI 包如何用 record 把完整 AXI 接口收敛为两个方向的记录（rec_axi_ms/rec_axi_sm），提升带 AXI 接口实体的可读性。
learning_goals: 理解 AXI 五通道的 record 建模
掌握 ms/sm 方向命名约定
知道默认常量与遗留 subtype
lectures.practice_task: 参照包中用法，声明一个 AXI 从机端口的 ms/sm 记录对并赋默认常量。

lectures.title: 简单双口 RAM：sdp_ram 与 sp_ram_be
lectures.topic: 讲解简单双口 RAM 的纯 VHDL 实现，包括 ram_style 综合属性、读前写/写前读（RBW/WBR）行为以及字节使能变体。
learning_goals: 理解 depth/width 与地址位宽关系
掌握 ram_style 与 RBW/WBR 行为差异
了解 shared variable 存储建模
lectures.practice_task: 实例化一个 512x16 的 sdp_ram，用 log2ceil 推导地址位宽，并说明 RBW 与 WBR 的输出差异。

lectures.title: 真双口 RAM：tdp_ram 与 tdp_ram_be
lectures.topic: 讲解真双口 RAM 的两个独立读写端口及其字节使能变体，说明它如何作为异步 FIFO 与乒乓缓冲的底层存储。
learning_goals: 理解真双口 RAM 的双端口模型
掌握字节使能写入
了解其作为跨时钟存储介质的作用
lectures.practice_task: 比较 sdp_ram 与 tdp_ram 的端口，说明跨时钟场景下为何应选 tdp_ram。

lectures.title: 同步 FIFO：sync_fifo
lectures.topic: 讲解同步 FIFO 的读写指针、满/空与几乎满/空标志、电平输出，以及它在单时钟域数据缓冲中的应用。
learning_goals: 理解 FIFO 满空判断原理
掌握 alm_full/alm_empty 与 level 输出
学会 AXI-S 接口的 FIFO 用法
lectures.practice_task: 实例化 sync_fifo，设计小场景观察 alm_full 在 depth=32 时的触发时刻。

lectures.title: 异步 FIFO：async_fifo 与格雷码指针
lectures.topic: 讲解异步 FIFO 如何用格雷码指针跨时钟域传递、如何区分满与空，以及多级寄存器同步与二进程 record 设计法。
learning_goals: 理解格雷码指针的 CDC 作用
掌握满/空判别（多一位指针）
了解二进程 record 架构
lectures.practice_task: 跟踪 async_fifo 中 WrAddrGray 到 RdAddrGraySync 的同步路径，解释为何需要格雷码。

lectures.title: 脉冲跨越 pulse_cc 与复位同步
lectures.topic: 讲解脉冲跨时钟域的

lectures.title: 数据/状态/位跨越 simple_cc/status_cc/bit_cc
lectures.topic: 讲解在 pulse_cc 之上构建的数据值跨越（simple_cc）、慢变状态跨越（status_cc）与单 bit 跨越（bit_cc）的设计与取舍。
learning_goals: 理解 simple_cc 复用 pulse_cc 的设计
区分脉冲/状态/位跨越的适用场景
学会复位输出传递

lectures.title: 同步整数比跨越 sync_cc_n2xn / sync_cc_xn2n
lectures.topic: 讲解两个同步（整数倍频）时钟域之间、带 AXI-S 握手的跨越，包括低到高（n2xn）与高到低（xn2n）两种方向。
learning_goals: 理解整数比时钟的同步跨越原理
掌握 n2xn/xn2n 的方向差异
了解 ratio 参数与节拍关系
lectures.practice_task: 给定 100MHz 与 25MHz 两个同步时钟，选择正确组件并预测数据聚合/分发行为。

lectures.title: 选通与节拍生成
lectures.topic: 讲解按频率生成单周期选通的 strobe_generator、按周期数可配置的 strobe_generator_cfg、节拍 tickgenerator 以及选通分频 strobe_divider。
learning_goals: 理解频率到计数比的换算
掌握 sync 同步输入
区分固定频率与可配置周期生成
理解选通分频
lectures.practice_task: 在 100MHz 时钟下用 strobe_generator 生成 1kHz 选通，再用 strobe_divider 分频 10 倍。

lectures.title: 时钟测量 clk_meas
lectures.topic: 讲解如何用已知参考时钟测量另一时钟（或选通）的频率，输出可读的频率/周期值。
learning_goals: 理解参考时钟计数测频原理
掌握 clk_meas 的接口与输出
了解其在系统自检中的用途
lectures.practice_task: 设计用 100MHz 参考测量一个未知时钟，并估算测量精度。

lectures.title: 流水线级 pl_stage 与二进程设计法
lectures.topic: 以 pl_stage 为例讲解带 AXI-S 握手的流水线级、影子寄存器处理反压，以及 PSI 库常用的二进程（record r/r_next）设计法。
learning_goals: 掌握二进程 record 设计法
理解影子寄存器如何处理反压
学会 use_rdy_g 的两种实现
lectures.practice_task: 在 pl_stage 中跟踪一次下游反压的数据流动，画出主/影寄存器状态。

lectures.title: 多级流水线 multi_pl_stage
lectures.topic: 讲解 multi_pl_stage 如何把多个 pl_stage 串联以打更深流水线，并统一处理握手与反压。
learning_goals: 理解多级流水线的级联结构
掌握 stages generic
了解反压在多级中的传递
lectures.practice_task: 实例化一个 4 级 multi_pl_stage，预测连续数据下的延迟周期数。

lectures.title: 可配置延迟 delay / delay_cfg
lectures.topic: 讲解固定延迟（delay，支持 BRAM/SRL/AUTO）与运行时可配置延迟（delay_cfg）的实现与资源选择。
learning_goals: 理解延迟线的存储实现
掌握 BRAM/SRL 资源选择
了解可配置延迟的寄存器接口
lectures.practice_task: 比较 delay 在 BRAM 与 SRL 两种 resource_g 下、不同 delay_g 的资源占用思路。

lectures.title: 乒乓缓冲 ping_pong
lectures.topic: 讲解乒乓缓冲如何用双 RAM 交替写入/读出实现连续数据流缓冲，及其 TDM 突发变体。
learning_goals: 理解乒乓双缓冲原理
掌握写/读切换逻辑
了解 TDM 突发应用

lectures.title: 数据宽度转换 wconv_n2xn / wconv_xn2n
lectures.topic: 讲解 N 位与整数倍 N 位之间的数据宽度转换，包括低到高聚合与高到低分发。
learning_goals: 理解宽度聚合/分发的握手时序
掌握 ratio 参数
了解与 sync_cc 的区别
lectures.practice_task: 把 8 位流聚合成 32 位流，预测输出 valid 的频率变化。

lectures.title: 并-TDM 与 TDM-并 par_tdm / tdm_par
lectures.topic: 讲解并行数据与时分复用（TDM）串行流之间的相互转换，以及隐式通道循环约定。
learning_goals: 理解 TDM 通道隐式循环
掌握 par_tdm/tdm_par 转换时序
了解 strobe 驱动
lectures.practice_task: 将 4 路并行 16 位数据转为 TDM 流并说明通道顺序。

lectures.title: 可配置 TDM 与多路复用 cfg/tdm_mux
lectures.topic: 讲解可配置有效通道数的 par_tdm_cfg/tdm_par_cfg，以及 TDM 多路复用器 tdm_mux。
learning_goals: 理解可配置通道数的 TDM 转换
掌握 tdm_mux 的复用与 str_del
了解代码生成器辅助
lectures.practice_task: 用 tdm_mux 把两路 TDM 流合并，并解释 str_del_g 的作用。

lectures.title: 并串/串并转换 par_ser / ser_par
lectures.topic: 讲解并行到串行与串行到并行的移位转换，包括 MSB/LSB 优先与速率比参数。
learning_goals: 理解并串/串并移位原理
掌握 msb/ratio 参数
了解与 strobe 的配合
lectures.practice_task: 用 par_ser 把 16 位并行数据按 MSB 优先、ratio=4 串行化。

lectures.title: SPI 主机 spi_master 与 spi_master_cfg
lectures.topic: 讲解 SPI 主机的时序（CPOL/CPHA）、位序与可配置位宽变体，以及三线 SPI 接口（spi_tri_o）。
learning_goals: 理解 CPOL/CPHA 四种模式
掌握 lsb_first 与位宽配置
了解三线 SPI 端口
lectures.practice_task: 配置 spi_master 为模式 1、MSB 优先，说明 SCLK 与片选时序。

lectures.title: I2C 主机 i2c_master
lectures.topic: 讲解 I2C 主机的 START/STOP/ACK 时序、时钟分频与内部三态选项。
learning_goals: 理解 I2C 协议帧时序
掌握时钟分频配置
了解 internal_tri_state 选项
lectures.practice_task: 描述一次 I2C 单字节写传输的 SDA/SCL 波形。

lectures.title: AXI 简化主机 axi_master_simple
lectures.topic: 讲解简化版 AXI4 主机如何把用户侧简单的命令/数据接口映射到 AXI 四通道握手。
learning_goals: 理解 AXI 四通道时序
掌握 simple 主机的命令接口
了解突发与 throttling
lectures.practice_task: 用 axi_master_simple 发起一次 4 拍突发读，画出握手时序。

lectures.title: AXI 完整主机 axi_master_full 与 axi_multi_pl_stage
lectures.topic: 讲解完整版 AXI 主机（支持完整特性）以及用于在 AXI 路径上插入多级流水线的 axi_multi_pl_stage。
learning_goals: 理解 full 与 simple 主机的差异
掌握 multi_pl_stage 对 AXI 的时序优化
了解 record 端口用法
lectures.practice_task: 说明在长布线 AXI 路径上插入 axi_multi_pl_stage 的收益。

lectures.title: AXI 从机 axi_slave_ipif 与 axilite_slave_ipif
lectures.topic: 讲解 AXI4/AXI-Lite 从机 IP 接口（IPIF）如何把寄存器/存储映射到 AXI，包括 32/64 位与 throttling。
learning_goals: 理解 IPIF 寄存器/存储映射
掌握 num_reg/use_mem 配置
了解 axi_throttling
lectures.practice_task: 用 axilite_slave_ipif 暴露 4 个寄存器，说明读写时序。

lectures.title: 仲裁器 arb_priority / arb_round_robin
lectures.topic: 讲解优先级仲裁与轮询（round robin）仲裁的请求/许可逻辑及其在多请求者总线中的应用。
learning_goals: 理解优先级与轮询仲裁算法
掌握请求/许可握手
了解公平性与延迟权衡
lectures.practice_task: 对 4 路同时请求，分别用两种仲裁器预测许可顺序。

lectures.title: 脉冲/斜坡生成与整形
lectures.topic: 讲解斜坡/脉冲发生器（ramp_gene、pulse_generator_ctrl_static）与脉冲整形器（pulse_shaper/cfg）的步进与定时参数。
learning_goals: 理解斜坡/脉冲步进参数
掌握整形器的 hold/hold-off
了解 strobe 驱动
lectures.practice_task: 用 ramp_gene 生成周期斜坡并说明步进参数含义。

lectures.title: 实用组件：看门狗/消抖/防优化/触发器/动态移位
lectures.topic: 讲解看门狗（watchdog）、消抖器（debouncer）、防综合优化（dont_opt）、模拟/数字触发生成（trigger_analog/digital）与动态移位器（dyn_sft）。
learning_goals: 理解看门狗与消抖原理
掌握 dont_opt 的综合用途
了解触发生成与动态移位
lectures.practice_task: 用 debouncer 处理一个按键输入，并选择合适的消抖周期。

lectures.title: 统计与信号源：min_max/prbs/pwm/sample_rate_converter
lectures.topic: 讲解 find_min_max/min_max_sum 流式统计、PRBS 伪随机序列、PWM 以及无滤波采样率转换器（sample_rate_converter）。
learning_goals: 理解流式最小/最大/求和
掌握 LFSR-PRBS 与 PWM
了解无滤波采样率转换原理
lectures.practice_task: 用 prbs 生成测试激励，并用 find_min_max 统计一段流的极值。

lectures.title: 编写自校验测试平台（psi_tb 用法）
lectures.topic: 以 pl_stage 测试平台为例，讲解 PSI 库自校验 TB 的结构、多进程协调、###ERROR### 约定与 psi_tb 工具包用法。
learning_goals: 掌握自校验 TB 结构

学会 psi_tb compare/txt_util 用法
lectures.practice_task: 仿照 pl_stage_tb 为一个简单组件搭建自校验 TB 框架。

lectures.title: Python 代码生成器 generators
lectures.topic: 讲解 generators 目录下用 Python 为 simple_cc/status_cc/par_tdm/tdm_par 生成特定位宽实例的脚本与 snippet 模板机制。

lectures.topic: 讲解向 PSI VHDL 库贡献代码的规范（代码质量、generic 化、自检 TB、文档）、git 分支策略、commit 注释约定与版本/标签管理。
learning_goals: 掌握贡献规范与检查清单

了解版本号与标签策略

lectures.title: 脉冲跨越 pulse_cc 与复位同步
lectures.topic: 讲解脉冲跨时钟域的翻转-同步-异或手法、复位双向同步，以及 ASYNC_REG/syn_srlstyle 等综合约束。

了解 CDC 综合属性
